title: festuca littorea
intro: Festuca littorea, also known as saltwort fescue, is a species of grass native to coastal areas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Festuca littorea can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
